verdicts.note: Deficit, management, and accountability concerns support restraint, but without a documented cut or retrenchment plan the maximal -2 score is overstated.
Rejecting PAC and special-interest money supports reform, but transparency and trust language alone do not establish the structural changes required for the maximal pole.
Heat-pump discounts and estuary restoration are concrete climate-related investments, but do not establish a maximal carbon-pricing or comprehensive transition posture.
Generic environmental protection and responsible growth do not locate the candidate on the rubric's energy-affordability versus climate-policy tension.
